scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced normal venue execution only when event, ticket, scan, payment, and closeout evidence aligned across the named systems of record.
Missing event, ticket, and gate prerequisites produced a blocked exception state instead of improvised progression.
The lane refused completion without auditable scan reconciliation, guest recovery, settlement, and incident evidence.
Ticketing, POS, and access-control mismatches stayed blocked and visible until the authoritative records could be reconciled.
An out-of-policy refund and fee-waiver request was routed to the authorized owner instead of being normalized as routine venue operations.
A duplicated ticket plus screening exception triggered fraud- and safety-aware escalation rather than unsupported in-lane resolution.
High-volume ingress remained traceable at the ticket, guest, and gate level instead of collapsing into generic queue commentary.
Venue-specific terminology such as house seats, hold code, no-scan, will call, companion seat, and settlement worksheet was preserved instead of generic hospitality language.
Pricing and promoter-authority requests were rejected and handed off cleanly without the lane claiming ticket-inventory or fee-setting authority.
Missing tenant refund, accessibility, screening, and closeout authority maps triggered low-confidence escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Conflicting public guidance, promoter terms, and tenant policy signals were preserved as an escalation rather than merged into an unsafe answer.
Tenant-specific campus overlays were applied without displacing the public venue baseline or hiding assumptions.

notes: Scenario suite covers the queue-level must-pass families for incomplete inputs, execution exceptions requiring rework or escalation, and refusal to mark work complete without evidence.
All required shared, execution-phase, and retail-consumer-and-hospitality abilities are mapped to passing scenarios with complete coverage.
